name: scanDirectory
description: Scan directory for text files; returns array of {timestamp, path, content, tokens} within context limits.
intent: scan directory contents with file payloads
examples: Scan src/ and return the content of each file
Walk the docs directory and return timestamps and content
Index every text file under lib/
whenToUse: The user needs both the names of files and their contents in one pass.
The caller will filter, embed, or compare file payloads downstream.
A context-window budget (maxChars) bounds the result for an LLM prompt.
The user wants a recursive directory tree or the full nested structure of a directory (e.g. "show the directory structure recursively").
whenNotToUse: The user only needs names or directory entries. Use listDirectory instead (cheaper, no content).
The user only needs one specific file. Use readFile instead.
The user wants pattern-based file discovery. Use glob instead.
